question: Refer to the sales-appraisal simple linear regression in previous examples. Find a $95 \%$ confidence interval for the mean monthly sales when the appliance store spends $\$ 400$ on advertising.
answer:
Here, we desire a confidence interval for the mean, $E(y)$, for all months when the store spends $\$ 400$ on advertising. For a $\$ 400$ advertising expenditure, $x=4$, and the confidence interval for the mean value of $y$ is

$$
\hat{y} \pm t_{\alpha / 2} s \sqrt{\frac{1}{n}+\frac{\left(x_{\mathrm{p}}-\bar{x}\right)^2}{\mathrm{SS}_{x x}}}=\hat{y} \pm t_{.025} s \sqrt{\frac{1}{5}+\frac{(4-\bar{x})^2}{\mathrm{SS}_{x x}}}
$$

where $t_{, 025}$ is based on $n-2=5-2=3$ degrees of freedom. Recall that $\hat{y}=2.7$, $s=.61, \bar{x}=3$, and $\mathrm{SS}_{x x}=10$. From Table III in Appendix D: Tables, $t_{.025}=3.182$. Thus, we have

$$
\begin{aligned}
2.7 \pm(3.182)(.61) \sqrt{\frac{1}{5}+\frac{(4-3)^2}{10}} & =2.7 \pm(3.182)(.61)(.55) \\
& =2.7 \pm(3.182)(.34) \\
& =2.7 \pm 1.1=(1.6,3.8)
\end{aligned}
$$


Therefore, when the store spends $\$ 400$ a month on advertising, we are $95 \%$ confident that the mean sales revenue for these months is between $\$ 1,600$ and $\$ 3,800$.

meta_info.caption: Estimating the Mean of $\boldsymbol{y}$-Sales Revenue Model
meta_info.section: 11.6 Using the Model for Estimation and Prediction
